name: Issue invoices with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive 24/7

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf03977-document`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Gather API Numbers

### Gather API Numbers

GET https://api.example.com/v1/documents — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Next: Blend Sources

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Gather from Google Drive

Searches Google Drive for matching files/folders — the discovery step before download or processing.

Next: Blend Sources

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Blend Sources

Rejoins 2 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Roll Up KPIs

### Roll Up KPIs

Group-by + aggregate (like a pivot table) — turns raw rows into KPI numbers.

Next: Compose HTML Report

### Compose HTML Report

Custom JavaScript: Build an HTML digest table from the aggregated rows

Next: Send Report via Gmail

### Send Report via Gmail

Sends an email via Gmail — subject is the first line of the message body, recipient comes from the item's `email` field.

Next: Save Execution Metadata

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2

### Save Execution Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Sort Documents Newest First

### Sort Documents Newest First

Orders items (newest first) so limits and digests pick the most relevant records.

# Issue invoices with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive 24/7

**Pattern:** Document Generation · **Level:** Foundation · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Issue invoices with HTTP / REST API and Google Drive 24/7. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual invoices is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Gather API Numbers → Blend Sources → Roll Up KPIs → Compose HTML Report → Send Report via Gmail → Save Execution Metadata → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to document generation without manual effort
- Anyone automating invoices in legal
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Scales without extra headcount
- Improves response time
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google OAuth2.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger — hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click → Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

KPIs are rolled up with Summarize (exact group-by + aggregate) before any AI touches the data — asking an LLM to 'figure out the numbers' from raw records is slower, costlier, and less reliable than doing exact arithmetic in n8n and only asking the model to narrate numbers that are already correct.

## Practice exercises

New to this pattern? Try these in order:

1. Add a second data source and watch it blend into the same report via Merge.
2. Toggle the AI executive-summary step on and off and compare the report's usefulness.
3. Change the KPI grouping field and see how the pivot reshapes.

## 🛟 When this fails

This starter workflow keeps things simple: no automatic retry or error branch yet. If a node fails, the execution just stops — open n8n's **Executions** tab to see which node and why. Ready to harden it? Turn on **Retry On Fail** (3 tries, 2s backoff) on the Settings tab of each external node, and add an error-output branch to a Slack/email alert — every Production-tier template in this library shows that exact pattern.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your invoices process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
